sensitivity: 93.9% (pneumothorax, balanced operating point); 94.3% (tension pneumothorax, balanced operating point)
specificity: 92.2% (pneumothorax, balanced operating point); 95.8% (tension pneumothorax, balanced operating point)
intended_use: The device is designed to aid the clinical assessment of adult chest x-ray cases with features suggestive of pneumothorax and tension pneumothorax in the medical care environment. The device analyses
intended_use_full: The device is designed to aid the clinical assessment of adult chest x-ray cases with features suggestive of pneumothorax and tension pneumothorax in the medical care environment. The device analyses cases using an artificial intelligence algorithm to identify findings. It makes case-level output available to a PACS or RIS for worklist prioritization or triage. The device is intended to be used by trained clinicians who are qualified to interpret chest X-rays as part of their scope of practice. The device is not intended to direct attention to specific portions of an image or to anomalies other than pneumothorax and tension pneumothorax. Its results are not intended to be used on a standalone basis for clinical decision making nor it is intended to rule out pneumothorax or tension pneumothorax, or otherwise preclude clinical assessment of X-ray cases.
ground_truth: Each deidentified CXR case was annotated in blinded fashion by at least two American Board of Radiology (ABR)-certified and protocol-trained radiologists, with consensus determined by two ground truthers and a third in event of disagreement.
other_metrics: Sensitivity and specificity reported for three operating points: 'optimized for sensitivity' (96.6% sensitivity, 84.1% specificity for pneumothorax; 95.9% sensitivity, 94.9% specificity for tension pneumothorax); 'optimized for specificity' (89.1% sensitivity, 95.7% specificity for pneumothorax; 83.7% sensitivity, 97.8% specificity for tension pneumothorax). Triage turn-around time: 20.57 seconds (95% CI: 19.90-21.24).
